summary: Completed comprehensive testing of enhanced AI Dependency Scanner features. Backend API fully functional with all enhanced data (frameworks_summary, hotspots, risk_flags, recommended_actions, pattern metadata, code snippets with [[[HIT]]] markers). Frontend Overview tab and Raw Data tab working correctly. Files tab has accordion expansion issue preventing display of enhanced occurrence metadata.

ui_bugs.issue: Accordion items not expanding properly to show occurrence details with enhanced metadata (severity badges, code snippets with highlighting)
ui_bugs.details: When clicking on file accordion items in Files tab, the content does not expand to show occurrences. The accordion trigger appears to work (data-state changes) but the AccordionContent with occurrence details (severity badges, pattern descriptions, code snippets) is not rendering. Backend data is correct and present in Raw Data tab.

passed_tests: Backend: Health check endpoint responding
Backend: Pattern configuration endpoint returns 7 frameworks with enhanced metadata
Backend: Invalid GitHub URL validation (400 error)
Backend: Valid repository scan completes successfully
Backend: Scan returns frameworks_summary with categories (client_init, llm_call, embedding_call, rag_pattern)
Backend: Scan returns hotspots showing directory concentration
Backend: Scan returns risk_flags with severity levels (high, medium, low)
Backend: Scan returns recommended_actions based on risk flags
Backend: Findings include pattern_category, pattern_severity, pattern_description
Backend: Code snippets include [[[HIT]]] markers for highlighting
Backend: GET /api/scans/{scan_id} endpoint retrieves scan with all enhanced data
Backend: GET /api/scans/non-existent-id returns 404
Frontend: Page loads successfully
Frontend: Scan form accepts GitHub URL and submits
Frontend: Scan completes and displays results
Frontend: Quick stats display (Total Files, Total Matches, Frameworks Found, Avg per File)
Frontend: Overview tab - Key Insights section displays with framework summary and usage types
Frontend: Overview tab - Risk Flags section displays with severity badges (amber/yellow for medium, blue for low)
Frontend: Overview tab - Framework Usage Breakdown section displays categories breakdown
Frontend: Overview tab - AI Hotspots table displays directory concentration
Frontend: Overview tab - Recommended Actions cards display with related risk flags
Frontend: Overview tab - Charts render (Occurrences by Framework, Files by Framework)
Frontend: Overview tab - Top Files table displays
Frontend: Files tab - File list renders with 100 files
Frontend: Files tab - Search and filter functionality present
Frontend: Raw Data tab - Accessible and displays JSON data
Frontend: Raw Data tab - Copy JSON button present and functional
Frontend: Raw Data tab - Download JSON button present and functional
Frontend: Raw Data tab - JSON contains frameworks_summary, hotspots, risk_flags, recommended_actions

success_percentage.frontend_files_tab: 50% (file list displays but accordion expansion not working)

items.description: The FileList component accordion items are not expanding properly to show occurrence details. When users click on a file, the accordion should expand to show all occurrences with enhanced metadata (severity badges, pattern category, pattern description, code snippets with yellow highlighting). Currently, the accordion trigger responds but the AccordionContent does not render the occurrence details.
debugging_hints: Check if occurrences data is being passed correctly to the FileList component
Verify Radix UI Accordion with type='multiple' is working correctly
Check if AccordionContent is rendering but hidden (CSS issue)
Verify that file.occurrences array has data when FileList renders
Check browser console for any React rendering errors
items.expected_behavior: When clicking on a file accordion item, it should expand to show a list of occurrences. Each occurrence should display: line number badge, framework badge, severity badge (with color coding), pattern category badge, pattern description text, and a code snippet with yellow highlighting on matched portions (marked with [[[HIT]]] in backend data).
